trig_12.label: Trigonometric Functions
misconceptions: Radians and degrees measure different things
trig_12.advanced: Analyzes sinusoidal functions and writes equations from graphs or descriptions. Applies transformations systematically and explains how each parameter affects the function. Solves trigonometric equations with multiple solutions and applies to real-world periodic phenomena.
trig_12.proficient: Evaluates trigonometric functions using the unit circle and reference angles. Identifies amplitude, period, and shifts from equations. Writes sinusoidal equations and graphs them accurately. Converts between degrees and radians.
trig_12.developing: Evaluates trigonometric functions with support. Identifies some transformations from graphs or equations. Converts between degrees and radians with reference materials.
trig_12.beginning: Knows basic trigonometric ratios. Cannot yet reliably use the unit circle or apply transformations independently.

trig_identity_12.label: Trigonometric Identities & Equations
misconceptions: An identity is proven by substituting one value
trig_identity_12.advanced: Verifies complex identities using multiple approaches. Derives sum and double-angle formulas from the unit circle. Solves trigonometric equations with multiple angles and identifies all solutions in given domains. Applies identities to prove additional relationships.
trig_identity_12.proficient: Verifies trigonometric identities using fundamental identities. Solves trigonometric equations and finds all solutions in a given interval. Applies double-angle and sum formulas. Explains the reasoning behind each step.
trig_identity_12.developing: Verifies simple identities with support. Solves trigonometric equations that lead to one or two solutions. Applies one identity at a time with guidance.
trig_identity_12.beginning: Recognizes basic trigonometric identities. Cannot yet verify identities or solve equations independently.

limits_12.label: Limits & Continuity
misconceptions: A limit must exist if a function is defined at a point
All discontinuities are of the same type
limits_12.advanced: Analyzes limits rigorously and explains continuity conceptually. Classifies discontinuities and explains why they occur. Determines limits at infinity and identifies asymptotes. Applies limits to understand function behavior near critical points.
limits_12.proficient: Evaluates limits algebraically and graphically. Determines if functions are continuous at a point. Classifies discontinuities (removable, jump, infinite). Finds horizontal and vertical asymptotes.
limits_12.developing: Evaluates limits for polynomial and rational functions with support. Identifies some discontinuities on graphs. Struggles to classify discontinuity types.
limits_12.beginning: Understands the concept of a limit. Cannot yet evaluate limits systematically or classify discontinuities independently.

probability_12.label: Probability & Combinatorics
misconceptions: Permutations and combinations are the same
All probabilities can be calculated using simple fractions
P(A and B) = P(A) × P(B) for any events
probability_12.advanced: Solves complex counting problems using multiple techniques. Calculates conditional probabilities and applies Bayes' theorem. Uses binomial and other probability models to solve real-world problems. Explains when different probability approaches are appropriate.
probability_12.proficient: Distinguishes between permutations and combinations and applies appropriately. Calculates probabilities for independent and dependent events. Uses binomial probability formula. Applies counting principles to complex situations.
probability_12.developing: Uses permutations and combinations with support. Calculates probabilities for independent events. Applies counting principles to standard problems.
probability_12.beginning: Recognizes permutations and combinations. Cannot yet calculate them reliably or apply probability formulas independently.

statistics_12.label: Statistical Inference
misconceptions: A 95% confidence interval means a 95% probability the true parameter is in that interval
A p-value is the probability the null hypothesis is true
Larger sample sizes always guarantee better estimates
statistics_12.advanced: Conducts full hypothesis tests with appropriate test statistics and interprets results correctly. Constructs confidence intervals and explains their meaning. Analyzes sampling methods and identifies sources of bias. Applies statistical inference to real-world data and explains limitations.
statistics_12.proficient: Calculates z-scores and uses normal distribution to find probabilities. Constructs confidence intervals and calculates margins of error. Conducts hypothesis tests and interprets p-values. Identifies biases in studies.
statistics_12.developing: Calculates z-scores and finds probabilities with support. Constructs confidence intervals with reference formulas. Performs basic hypothesis tests with guidance.
statistics_12.beginning: Understands basic statistical concepts. Cannot yet independently calculate or interpret confidence intervals and hypothesis tests.
